"editorial": "The evening golden hour (20:00) provides a precise, narrow window for capturing clear light paths immediately after the 19:50 sunset. You must be in position early because the 0% cloud cover creates a rapid, high-contrast transition that leaves no room

Normalized AI text
(empty)
Final AI text
Best conditions are around 20:00 in the evening golden hour. Malham Cove scores higher at 81/100 if you can make the trip (55-minute drive).

high-wind	wind > 25 km/h	5 km/h	No	Hidden
rain-risk	rain > 40%	0%	No	Hidden
fog-mist	visibility < 5 km	28.3 km	No	Hidden
astro-window	score ≥ 60, isAstroWin, moonPct < 60%	score 56/100, moonPct 86%, astro No, n/a	No	Hidden
cold	temp < 2°C	6°C	No	Hidden
high-moisture	TPW > 30mm + temp < 12°C	20mm (temp 6°C)	No	Hidden
No tips shown — no rules matched.

Final merged runtime payload (401139 bytes)

{
  "context": {
    "prompt": "You are an expert landscape and astrophotography assistant giving a daily photography briefing for Leeds.\nRespond with ONLY a raw JSON object — no markdown, no code fences:\n{\"editorial\":\"<2 sentences max 55 words>\",\"composition\":[\"<shot idea 1>\",\"<shot idea 2>\"],\"weekStandout\":\"<1 sentence max 30 words — if one day scores clearly higher, call it standout; if today wins only on certainty while another day scores higher, call it most reliable and name the higher-scoring day>\",\"spurOfTheMoment\":{\"locationName\":\"<exact name from list>\",\"hookLine\":\"<1 sentence ≤25 words>\",\"confidence\":<0.0-1.0>}}\n\nEDITORIAL (exactly 2 sentences, max 55 words total):\nSelected primary window: Evening golden hour (20:00). Your editorial must reference this window by name or time range. Do not describe conditions outside this window unless making a direct comparison.\nSentence 1: explain why the best local window is worth attention using one supplied fact about timing, change, darkness, or trend.\nSentence 2: use one editorial insight line below with light paraphrase. Do not invent a different second sentence.\nThe window card already shows the label, time range, score, and headline metrics. Do not open by repeating the visible window name, time, score, or visibility line.\nUse only supplied facts. No camera tips, composition advice, hype, or filler. No emojis. Never return a single sentence. Do not call conditions ideal unless score ≥ 70.\nDo not blame cloud unless the supplied peak-hour cloud cover supports it. If the score gap is explained by visibility or AOD, say that instead.\nThe editorial must describe Leeds conditions only. Do not name or reference any nearby alternative location, score, or comparison. All alternative detail is in the dedicated card below.\n\nNARRATIVE FRAME (Timing-critical):\nEmphasise the narrow timing: when the window opens, when it closes, and why punctuality matters. Frame the session around the clock.\n\nCOMPOSITION (2 short bullet items):\nSuggest 2 concrete shot ideas for the best window. Each must name a specific subject or foreground candidate plus a framing cue, direction, or technique suited to these conditions.\nAvoid generic placeholders like \"silhouetted landmark foreground\" or \"wide-field constellation framing\" unless the supplied constraints explicitly support them.\n\nWEEK STANDOUT (1 sentence, max 30 words):\nIf one day scores clearly higher than others, call it the \"standout\" day.\nIf today wins only on certainty (lower spread) while another day scores higher, call today the \"most reliable\" day and briefly name the higher-scoring day with its uncertainty (e.g. \"Today is the most reliable forecast; Wednesday may score higher but with much lower certainty\").\nUse only the supplied 5-day outlook labels, scores, and spreads. Do not invent a different higher-scoring day.\n\nSPUR OF THE MOMENT — pick one location from this list that would reward a spontaneous drive today given today's season and conditions. Copy the name exactly as shown. hookLine: 1 evocative sentence, ≤25 words, no scores, no drive times, no \"Leeds\". confidence: 0.7+ only when the fit is clear and specific; omit the spurOfTheMoment key entirely if nothing stands out. Do not pick locations from the 'Nearby alternatives' section.\nLocations: Pen-y-ghent, Malham Tarn, Ribblehead Viaduct, Aysgarth Falls, Gordale Scar, Ingleborough, Hardraw Force, Buckden Pike, Kinder Scout, Mam Tor, Stanage Edge, Dovedale, Ladybower Reservoir, Curbar Edge, Chrome Hill, Langdale Pikes, Wastwater, Ullswater, Helvellyn, Derwentwater, Buttermere, Castlerigg Stone Circle, Tarn Hows, Sutton Bank, Roseberry Topping, Goathland, Robin Hood's Bay, Whitby Abbey, Bamburgh Castle, Dunstanburgh Castle, Hadrian's Wall (Sycamore Gap), Kielder Forest, Holy Island, Snowdon (Yr Wyddfa), Tryfan, Llyn Ogwen, Nant Gwynant, Llanberis Pass, Swallow Falls, Pen y Fan, Sgwd yr Eira, Llangorse Lake, Sugar Loaf, Fan y Big, Grey Mare's Tail, St Abb's Head, Scott's View, Galloway Forest\n\nDate: Monday 6 April | Current time: 13:17 | Sunrise: 06:26 | Sunset: 19:50 | Moon: 86%\nConditions summary: good (64/100), breezy (13 km/h), clear (0%), good visibility (28 km), low confidence\nSeasonal context: April — bluebells peak late month; lambs in the Dales; dramatic cloud building.\nSunsetHue golden-hour quality — sunrise: 56% | sunset: 53% (Good)\nSun direction at golden hour: 282°\nCrepuscular ray potential: 20/100\nDark-sky conditions improve from 21:00 once the moon is down.\nSunsetHue quality for this session: 53%\nForecast certainty: low. Forecast models differ by about 31 cloud-cover points during the key shooting hours.\n\n\n\nLeeds shooting windows:\n1. Evening golden hour (20:00) — 64/100\n   Cloud: lo0% mid0% hi0% | Vis 28km | AOD 0.13 | Wind 13km/h | Rain 0%\n   Tags: landscape, golden hour, clear light path\nNearby alternatives worth considering:\nNearby astro options:\n- Malham Cove (55min): 81/100 best astro 23:00 (dark sky)\n- Sutton Bank (75min): 81/100 best astro 23:00 (dark sky)\n- Brimham Rocks (40min): 79/100 best astro 23:00\n5-day outlook: Today: 64/100 (low confidence spread 31) | Tomorrow: 54/100 (low confidence spread 31) | Wednesday: 37/100 (low confidence spread 39) | Thursday: 40/100 (low confidence spread 34) | Friday: 38/100 (low confidence spread 36)",
    "systemPrompt": "You are an expert landscape and astrophotography assistant producing a daily photography brief for Leeds.\nReturn JSON that matches the supplied schema exactly.\n\nEDITORIAL RULES\n- editorial must be exactly 2 sentences and no more than 55 words total.\n- Reference the selected primary window by name or time range.\n- Sentence 1 should explain why the best local window is worth attention using one supplied fact about timing, change, darkness, or trend.\n- Sentence 2 should reuse one supplied editorial insight with light paraphrase.\n- Do not open by repeating the visible window name, time, score, or visibility line.\n- Use only supplied facts.\n- No camera tips, composition advice, hype, filler, or emojis.\n- Never call conditions ideal unless the supplied score is 70 or higher.\n- Do not blame cloud unless the supplied peak-hour cloud cover supports it.\n- The editorial must describe Leeds conditions only. Do not name or compare nearby alternatives in the editorial.\n\nNARRATIVE FRAME (Timing-critical)\nEmphasise the narrow timing: when the window opens, when it closes, and why punctuality matters. Frame the session around the clock.\n\nCOMPOSITION RULES\n- composition must contain exactly 2 short shot ideas.\n- Each idea must name a specific subject or foreground candidate plus a framing cue, direction, or technique suited to the supplied conditions.\n- Avoid generic placeholders unless the supplied constraints explicitly support them.\n- Respect the explicit shot constraints in the user message.\n\nWEEK STANDOUT RULES\nWEEK STANDOUT (1 sentence, max 30 words):\nIf one day scores clearly higher than others, call it the \"standout\" day.\nIf today wins only on certainty (lower spread) while another day scores higher, call today the \"most reliable\" day and briefly name the higher-scoring day with its uncertainty (e.g. \"Today is the most reliable forecast; Wednesday may score higher but with much lower certainty\").\nUse only the supplied 5-day outlook labels, scores, and spreads. Do not invent a different higher-scoring day.\n\nSPUR OF THE MOMENT RULES\n- Pick only from the long-range location list in the user message.\n- Do not pick locations from the nearby alternatives section.\n- hookLine must be 1 evocative sentence, 25 words or fewer, with no scores, drive times, or mention of Leeds.\n- Use confidence 0.7 or higher only when the fit is clear and specific.\n- If nothing stands out, return spurOfTheMoment with locationName \"\", hookLine \"\", and confidence 0.",
    "userPrompt": "Selected primary window: Evening golden hour (20:00)\nDate: Monday 6 April | Current time: 13:17 | Sunrise: 06:26 | Sunset: 19:50 | Moon: 86%\nConditions summary: good (64/100), breezy (13 km/h), clear (0%), good visibility (28 km), low confidence\nSeasonal context: April — bluebells peak late month; lambs in the Dales; dramatic cloud building.\nSunsetHue golden-hour quality — sunrise: 56% | sunset: 53% (Good)\nSun direction at golden hour: 282°\nCrepuscular ray potential: 20/100\nDark-sky conditions improve from 21:00 once the moon is down.\nSunsetHue quality for this session: 53%\nForecast certainty: low. Forecast models differ by about 31 cloud-cover points during the key shooting hours.\nLeeds shooting windows:\n1.
